description: Forward, pre-registered SIGN-predictions where the geometry and scalar economics diverge on a non-monetary coordinate. Frozen + hashed before resolution (registry.lock.json). Resolutions appended later without altering the frozen block.

frozen.claim: Introducing a monetary fine or payment into a domain with high pre-existing social-norm / identity salience (e.g. blood, organ, volunteering, civic compliance, prosocial conservation) will worsen the target behaviour relative to a no-incentive control, at least at low incentive levels.
frozen.scalar_prediction: target behaviour improves monotonically with the incentive
frozen.geometry_prediction: target behaviour reverses (sign flip) as d1 displaces d6/d7
frozen.resolution_criterion: any future field experiment/policy with a no-incentive control in a high-norm domain; scored on the SIGN of the treatment effect on the target behaviour
frozen.falsifier: improvement (correct scalar sign) with no crowding-out at low incentive

frozen.claim: Following a widely-perceived legitimacy shock to a tax or regulatory authority (rates/penalties unchanged), voluntary compliance falls relative to its pre-shock trend.
frozen.geometry_prediction: compliance falls with the legitimacy drop on d8
frozen.resolution_criterion: identified legitimacy shocks (contested elections, scandals, rulings) with unchanged monetary terms; scored on the SIGN of the compliance change vs trend
frozen.falsifier: no compliance change after a clear legitimacy shock with fixed incentives

frozen.claim: At identical monetary cost to households, a carbon price framed as a civic 'fee-and-dividend' (identity/legitimacy frame) secures higher public acceptance than the same price framed as a 'tax' (transaction frame).
frozen.geometry_prediction: civic frame raises acceptance via d7/d8
frozen.resolution_criterion: referenda, surveys, or field trials varying the frame at matched monetary cost; scored on the SIGN of the acceptance difference
frozen.falsifier: no acceptance difference between frames at equal cost

frozen.claim: When a probabilistic risk is communicated with explicit ambiguity ('experts disagree', 'estimate uncertain') versus a precise point probability of identical stated value, uptake of the protective/precautionary option rises under the ambiguous framing (loss domain) even though the stated probability is unchanged.
frozen.scalar_prediction: identical choice (stated probability and payoffs unchanged)
frozen.geometry_prediction: epistemic ambiguity shifts choice via d9 (loss-domain flip)
frozen.resolution_criterion: public-health / insurance / warning-label field or survey experiments holding stated probability fixed while varying epistemic framing; scored on the SIGN of the uptake difference
frozen.falsifier: no uptake difference when stated probability is held fixed
